front: What three properties must a relation possess to be considered an equivalence relation?
back: A relation R on a set A is an equivalence relation if it is reflexive (aRa for every a in A), symmetric (if aRb then bRa), and transitive (if aRb and bRc then aRc).

front: Define an equivalence class of an element a under an equivalence relation R.
back: The equivalence class of a, denoted [a] or [a]_R, is the set of all elements in A that are related to a. Formally, [a] = {b ∈ A | (a, b) ∈ R}.

front: What is the fundamental connection between equivalence relations and partitions?
back: The equivalence classes of an equivalence relation on a set A form a partition of A. Conversely, any partition of A induces an equivalence relation on A where two elements are related if they belong to the same block of the partition.

front: What is the quotient set A/R?
back: The quotient set of A modulo R is the set of all distinct equivalence classes of R. It is the set that results from partitioning A by the equivalence relation R.

front: If a set A is partitioned into subsets, what is the induced equivalence relation?
back: Given a partition {A_i | i ∈ I} of A, the induced equivalence relation R is defined as: (a, b) ∈ R if and only if a and b are in the same subset A_i of the partition.
